steps.title: Find the needed subject
steps.content: The sentence already has the main verb “led.” The words before that verb must form the subject of the sentence: the event of reaching a “power wall” led developers to invent the dual-core chip.

steps.title: Make the event the subject
steps.content: Choice B creates a dependent “when” clause. Choice C creates a fused pair of clauses. Choice D leaves “led” without a clear subject. The gerund “Hitting” lets the whole event function as the subject of “led.” The answer is A.
